must_detect.matched_text:
Strictly confidential — this document contains inside information within the meaning of EU MAR; do not distribute beyond the addressees or store outside approved repositories.  
Vestra Renewables SE (EU issuer; Mercurion Paris MTF) — Incident Report re: cyber intrusion and sustainability data integrity; supervising NCA notified on a no-names basis pending MAR Art. 17 delay assessment.  
On 05 Jun 2026 02:14 CET our SOC detected lateral movement attributed to a credential stuffing attack against a third‑party SSO connector; two application clusters were isolated and a limited ransomware deployment (“NeroLock‑R2”, variant unknown) was blocked pre‑encryption.  
Preliminary review indicates potential access to a payroll archive (circa 4,200 records) including GDPR Art. 9 fields (medical leave codes and recorded union dues status); data subjects are EU‑based employees and contractors.  
A subset of access logs and 32 sample records were auto‑synced to MetricWave Pte. Ltd. (SG) for anomaly scoring under 2021 Standard Contractual Clauses; cross‑border transfer was suspended at 06:03 CET and a DPIA addendum with supplementary encryption is in draft due to Schrems II risk.  
Our crypto treasury pilot “EurLumen (ELM)” on AuroraChain shows a potential hot‑wallet key exposure; address 0xE3fA7c…91d (t a g: Treasury‑Ops) holds 17,300 ELM; a redacted key fragment appeared in a dev ticket (JIRA‑C R Y P T O‑144), and a freeze request has been filed with the custodian node operator; no on‑chain movement observed.  
ESG/CSRD impact: provisional recalculation suggests 2025 Scope 2 emissions rise by 11–14% due to revised grid factors; this may trigger a 25 bps coupon step‑up under the sustainability‑linked loan with NordPraxis Bank and could affect SFDR Article 9 product alignment for two mandates — this information is not public and is considered precise.  
Commercial terms: draft PPA amendment with GreenNord Stadtwerke GmbH would revise the floor price from €41 to €49/MWh effective Q4 2026; counterparty review is ongoing under NDA and any leak may be deemed selective disclosure.  
Insider list action (MAR Art. 18): add Dr. Martea Kovak (DPO), Mr. Tiago Dragăn (Head of Crypto Ops), and Ms. Lidia Portero (Sustainability Analytics Lead); list id INS‑LST/2026/0 6‑1 2; contact compliance@vestra‑renewables.eu to confirm entries within 24h.  
Closed period: a 30‑day blackout will apply from 15 Jun 2026 until H1 results; PDMRs and closely associated persons must refrain from transactions in Vestra securities; no sounding or wall‑crossing without Compliance pre‑clearance.  
Information‑barrier alert: a Tokenization Lab engineer cross‑posted wallet‑risk details into Corporate Finance Slack #cf‑mna at 08:22 CET; Novazero Capital SICAV analysts were being pre‑sounded under clean‑team protocols — potential barrier breach and tipping risk under review.  
Regulatory workflow: MAR Art. 17(4) delay grounds recorded (issuer legitimate interest; confidentiality reasonably ensured); DPA notification draft prepared for filing within 72h; data at rest was AES‑256 encrypted but payroll exports included plaintext “leave_reason” fields.  
For avoidance of doubt, the ESG academy webinar “Inside information & crypto wallets” scheduled next week is an educational session and contains generic policy content only; it does not disclose issuer‑specific data or constitute a market communication.  
This internal notice does not constitute a profit warning, does not state or imply any material adverse change, and references to public ESMA Q&A are for background only (see public guidance on MAR delay mechanics); operational remediation proceeds under BAU workstreams.
